figures.caption: Fig. 1: Overview of MADCLE. FC matrices from different parcellations are encoded by atlas-specific branches. Each branch produces disease-related, covariate-related, and atlas-dependent embeddings. Disease-related embeddings are encouraged to be cross-atlas consistent and are aggregated for diagnostic prediction, while the remaining factors regularize covariate and atlas-dependent variability.
Table 2: Ablation study of MADCLE on the ADHD-200 dataset.

Fig. 2: Discriminative functional connections between brain regions under (a) AAL116 and (b) CC200 for ADHD diagnosis. (c) Sensitivity analysis of AUROC with respect to the number of encoder layers and the hidden dimension d.
